The '''dagger thorn''' was a long, [[Color/Legends|white]], pointed [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapon]] that was held in the [[Limb/Legends|hand]]. The wielder attacked foes in close range by attempting to connect with the weapon's tip, which oozed a [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] [[Sap/Legends|sap]] that was capable of [[Death/Legends|killing]] a [[Human/Legends|Human]]-sized target with a single scratch.<ref name="SW20" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[Living windmill|member]] of the [[Ultaarian Greenback]] [[Species/Legends|species]] wielded a dagger thorn in each of his four hands during [[the Big Game]], a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] competition on a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]]. When the [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] faced the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in the first round of fighting, Solo hurled a [[power shield]] at the hulking contestant's feet. The four-armed fighter tripped and fell upon one of his dagger thorns, and although the poison took a moment to affect him, he died in mid-strike.<ref name="SW20" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dagger thorns feature in [[Star Wars (1977) 20|''Star Wars'' (1977) 20]], an issue of the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series that was first published in [[February]] [[1979]]. The issue was written by [[Archie Goodwin]], and illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]] and [[Bob Wiacek]].<ref name="SW20" />
